summary: Alpha8 GPU surface returns incorrect pixel values (5 instead of 50) when cleared with SKColor(25, 0, 0, 50), while CPU surface returns the correct alpha value; reporter confirms the issue also reproduces in native C++ Skia.

stepsToReproduce: Create a GPU-backed SKSurface using SKSurface.Create(context, false, imageInfo)
Snapshot the surface, convert to bitmap via SKBitmap.FromImage
Read the pixel bytes — expect alpha=50 but get alpha=5

repoLinks.description: Skia fiddle showing expected Alpha8 behavior

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: No PRs or fixes found for Alpha8 GPU readback. The GPU surface creation and readback code paths haven't changed for Alpha8 handling.

analysis.summary: GPU-backed Alpha8 surface returns wrong pixel value (5 instead of 50) when cleared with SKColor(25, 0, 0, 50). The value 5 is suspiciously close to premultiplied R (25 * 50/255 ≈ 4.9), suggesting the GPU backend may be storing the premultiplied red channel as the alpha value instead of preserving the alpha channel directly. Reporter confirms the issue also reproduces in native C++ Skia, indicating this is an upstream Skia bug rather than a SkiaSharp wrapper issue.
analysis.rationale: Classified as type/bug because GPU and CPU render paths produce different pixel values for the same Alpha8 clear operation — this is incorrect behavior, not a usage question. Area is area/SkiaSharp because the reporter uses core SKSurface/SKImage APIs, though the root cause is likely in upstream Skia's GPU backend. The backend is OpenGL since this is WPF on Windows (default GPU backend). Severity is medium because there is a clear workaround (use CPU surfaces for Alpha8) and the use case is somewhat niche.

keySignals.interpretation: GPU readback returns alpha=5 instead of expected alpha=50 for Alpha8 surface.
CPU surface correctly returns alpha=50, proving the expected value and isolating the bug to GPU path.

keySignals.text: if i test this in pure native i get SOMETHING ELSE
keySignals.interpretation: Bug reproduces in native C++ Skia, confirming this is an upstream Skia issue, not a SkiaSharp wrapper bug.

codeInvestigation.finding: sk_surface_new_render_target passes through to SkSurfaces::RenderTarget with no Alpha8-specific handling — the GPU surface creation delegates entirely to upstream Skia.

SKBitmap.FromImage uses SKImageInfo.PlatformColorType (BGRA8888) instead of preserving the source image's color type. This causes Alpha8 images to be read as BGRA8888, producing 4 bytes per pixel instead of 1 — a secondary issue that affects the output format but not the core alpha value discrepancy.
ReadPixels delegates to sk_image_read_pixels which calls upstream Skia. No Alpha8-specific conversion or handling in the wrapper.

workarounds: Use CPU-backed surfaces for Alpha8 rendering: SKSurface.Create(imageInfo) instead of SKSurface.Create(context, budgeted, imageInfo)
nextQuestions: Does this reproduce on other GPU backends (Metal, Vulkan, Direct3D)?
Has this been fixed in newer Skia milestones (post-m103)?
Is the value 5 specifically premultiplied-R (25 * 50/255 ≈ 4.9) or is the calculation different?

resolution.hypothesis: The GPU backend misinterprets color channels when clearing or reading back Alpha8 surfaces. The returned value (5) matches premultiplied red (25 * 50/255 ≈ 4.9), suggesting the GPU stores the premultiplied R channel where the alpha should go.

proposals.title: Use CPU surface for Alpha8
proposals.description: Use SKSurface.Create(imageInfo) instead of SKSurface.Create(context, budgeted, imageInfo) when working with Alpha8 color type. CPU surfaces handle Alpha8 correctly.

proposals.title: Report upstream to Skia
proposals.description: File a bug against upstream Skia for Alpha8 GPU surface readback producing incorrect values. Reporter confirmed the issue exists in native C++ Skia as well.

resolution.recommendedReason: Trivial change that avoids the GPU code path entirely. The reporter's use case (1x1 Alpha8 pixel) doesn't benefit from GPU acceleration.

actionability.reason: Well-documented bug with complete repro code, confirmed to also affect upstream Skia. Needs investigation to determine if newer Skia versions fix this or if an upstream bug report is needed.

actions.description: Acknowledge bug, provide CPU workaround, explain upstream nature
actions.comment:
Thanks for the thorough investigation, including testing at the native C++ level — that's very helpful.

Since you've confirmed this also reproduces in native Skia, the root cause is in Skia's GPU backend handling of Alpha8 surfaces, not in the SkiaSharp wrapper. The returned value (5) closely matches premultiplied red (25 × 50/255 ≈ 4.9), which suggests the GPU path is storing a premultiplied color channel rather than the alpha directly.

As a workaround, you can use a CPU-backed surface for Alpha8 rendering:

```csharp
// Use CPU surface instead of GPU for Alpha8
using var surface = SKSurface.Create(imageInfo);
```

This avoids the GPU readback path entirely and correctly returns the expected alpha value. We'll track this for investigation against the upstream Skia library.
